questionHtml: State the equation for the reaction of calcium carbonate, the active ingredient in some antacids, with stomach acid.
markschemeHtml:
CaCO3 (s) + 2HCl (aq) → CaCl2 (aq) + H2O (l) + CO2 (g)

OR

CaCO3 (s) + 2H+ (aq) → Ca2+ (aq) + H2O (l) + CO2 (g) ✔

meta.title: State the equation for the reaction of calcium carbonate, the active ingredient in some antacids, with stomach acid.
breadcrumbLabels: D.4 pH regulation of the stomach
